steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The blank joins two independent clauses: ‘Compiling isn’t exclusive to certain programming languages’ and ‘any language can incorporate this tool,’ with ‘however’ marking a contrast. Two valid placements exist for the conjunctive adverb: ending IC1 as a trailing parenthetical (‘, however;’) or starting IC2 (‘; however,’). Test which placement the sentence prefers.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "languages; however": Missing the comma after “however” at the start of the second independent clause. ✗
  • "languages, however,": Comma before and comma after “however” leaves only commas separating two independent clauses — a comma splice. ✗
  • "languages. However": Missing the comma after “However” at the start of the second sentence. ✗
  • "languages, however;": The comma binds “however” to the first independent clause as a trailing parenthetical sentence adverb, and the semicolon correctly joins the next independent clause. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
